Tool_credit_card_emi_Description: Convert to EMI vs minimum payment trap. No-Cost EMI reality check.
Tool_credit_card_emi_LearnText: Convert a purchase into EMI using the reducing-balance formula. Enter a monthly or annual rate — many cards quote monthly %. Compare with the flat-rate method dealers sometimes use, so you see the real interest cost.
Tool_credit_card_emi_FormulaNote: Reducing EMI uses standard amortisation. Flat interest = P × annual% × years. “No-cost EMI” often hides interest in a higher MRP or processing fee.
Tool_credit_card_emi_VerdictBody: Reducing EMI ≈ {0}/month with about {1} interest. Flat method would charge about {2} interest on the same quote.
Tool_credit_card_emi_ExampleStep_1: Purchase ₹50,000; tenure 12 months; monthly rate 1.5% → 18% p.a.
Tool_credit_card_emi_ExampleStep_2: Reducing EMI uses the standard formula on outstanding balance.
Tool_credit_card_emi_ExampleStep_3: Total payment = EMI × 12; interest = total − principal.
Tool_credit_card_emi_ExampleStep_5: Compare flat vs reducing to spot the higher flat cost.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Step_1: Enter purchase amount and tenure in months.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Step_2: Enter monthly or annual rate (convert monthly × 12).
Tool_credit_card_emi_Step_3: Compute reducing EMI, total interest, and total payment.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Step_4: Compare with flat-rate interest and EMI.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Abbreviation: CC EMI — purchase to equated monthly instalments
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Purpose: Estimate EMI, interest, and flat vs reducing cost on a card purchase.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_WhyKnow: Card EMI and “no-cost” offers can hide high effective rates. Comparing methods protects you.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_RealLife: Converting a phone or appliance purchase to EMI; checking a no-cost EMI quote.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Goal_3: Use monthly or annual rate input
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Alt_Flat_When: When you only need a side-by-side flat vs reducing compare.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Alt_Emi_When: For a generic loan EMI without flat compare.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Alt_Personal_W: When financing via a personal loan instead of card EMI.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra shows reducing EMI plus a flat comparison and monthly/annual rate toggle for card purchases.
Tool_credit_card_emi_Info_Motto: Know the real EMI cost — not just the monthly number.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Description: Find hidden interest rate in your EMI. No-cost EMI reality check.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Subtitle: Reverse-engineer the interest rate from EMI
Tool_emi_to_rate_LearnText: You know the loan amount, EMI, and tenure — but not the rate. This tool solves the reducing-balance EMI formula for the implied annual interest rate. Useful for “no-cost EMI” and opaque dealer quotes.
Tool_emi_to_rate_FormulaNote: Uses binary search on the standard EMI formula. If EMI × months is less than principal, the schedule is impossible at a non-negative rate.
Tool_emi_to_rate_MonthsHint: Use the EMI tenure from your statement or offer letter.
Tool_emi_to_rate_VerdictBody: Your EMI implies about {0} p.a., with roughly {1} total interest over the tenure.
Tool_emi_to_rate_VerdictImpossible: EMI × months is less than principal — that schedule cannot amortise at a non-negative interest rate. Check the inputs.
Tool_emi_to_rate_ScenarioSubtitle: How a slightly different EMI changes the implied rate
Tool_emi_to_rate_ExampleStep_4: Binary-search annual rate until reducing EMI matches ₹4,800.
Tool_emi_to_rate_ExampleStep_5: The solved rate is the effective interest baked into the offer.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Step_1: Enter principal, monthly EMI, and tenure months.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Step_2: If EMI ≤ principal / months, rate is 0% (or impossible if lower).
Tool_emi_to_rate_Step_3: Binary-search annual rate so computed EMI matches input.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Step_4: Show implied rate, total interest, and total payment.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Abbreviation: EMI to rate — reverse interest calculator
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Purpose: Find the annual interest rate implied by a given EMI schedule.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_WhyKnow: Offers often hide the rate. Knowing the implied APR helps compare with bank loans.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_RealLife: No-cost EMI on electronics; dealer finance; checking a loan offer letter.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Alt_Emi_When: When you know the rate and want EMI.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Alt_Flat_When: When comparing flat and reducing quotes at a known rate.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Alt_Cc_When: When converting a purchase to EMI with a known rate.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra inverts the EMI formula to reveal the effective annual rate hidden in an offer.
Tool_emi_to_rate_Info_Motto: If they won’t quote the rate — reverse it from the EMI.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Description: Save by switching lender. Break-even months, top-up option, transfer verdict.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Subtitle: Current loan vs new rate after transfer fee
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_LearnText: A balance transfer moves your outstanding to a lower-rate lender. You pay a processing fee, then a new EMI. This tool compares remaining interest, net savings after fee, and break-even months until the fee is recovered.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_FormulaNote: Assumes same outstanding and remaining tenure on both loans. Check foreclosure charges, legal fees, and whether the new lender resets tenure.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_FeeHint: Typical BT fee is about 0.5–2% of outstanding (illustrative).
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_VerdictYes: Transfer looks worthwhile — about {0} net savings. Fee recovers in roughly {1} months via lower EMI.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_VerdictNo: Fee of {0} may wipe out interest savings of {1}. Re-check rates, tenure, and other charges.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_ExampleStep_2: Compute current EMI and remaining interest.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_ExampleStep_3: New rate 8.2% → new EMI and interest; fee = 1% of outstanding.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_ExampleStep_5: Break-even months ≈ fee ÷ (current EMI − new EMI).
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Step_1: Enter outstanding, current rate, and remaining months.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Step_2: Enter new rate and processing fee %.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Step_4: Net save = interest saved − fee; break-even ≈ fee / monthly EMI save.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_Purpose: Compare staying vs transferring: interest, fee, net savings, break-even.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_WhyKnow: A lower rate is not always worth it after fees. Break-even months show how long you must stay.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_RealLife: Home or personal loan balance transfer offers from banks and NBFCs.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_Alt_Prep: When you have a lump sum to cut principal instead of switching lender.
₹5 lakh prepay — reduce EMI vs tenure.
Needs cash; may have prepay charges.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_Alt_Home: To recompute EMI on a single rate and tenure.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_Differen: FinYatra nets processing fee against interest saved and shows break-even months for a transfer decision.
Tool_loan_balance_transfer_Info_Motto: Lower rate only wins after the fee pays for itself.
